metadata.title: Glagolitic / Old Church Slavonic Lexicon
metadata.description: Expanded and normalized Glagolitic / Old Church Slavonic dataset derived from glagolitic_lexicon.json; preserves original records while adding source-indexed attestation resources and unified lexical fields.
metadata.compiled_by: Lackadaisical Security (The Operator) - Spectre (GPT) - Aurora (Sonnet) - STONEDRIFT 3000

unicode_code_charts.title: The Unicode Consortium – Code Charts
unicode_code_charts.scope: script sign/codepoint verification across encoded scripts

linguistic_principles.normalization: Original fields are preserved under original_record; normalized fields are added for common programmatic access.
linguistic_principles.attestation_policy: Specific attestations are retained when present in the seed file. Where entry-level attestations are absent, entries are linked to reputable resource indexes for manual verification rather than inventing inscriptional claims.
linguistic_principles.confidence_policy: Seed confidence values are preserved when present. Default confidence 0.80 indicates normalized-but-not-entry-verified.
linguistic_principles.polysemy_policy: Definitions are arrays to permit multiple meanings, semantic drift, and context-dependent collapse.

metadata.historical_significance: Earliest Slavic alphabetic tradition used for Old Church Slavonic liturgical texts.

notes.compiler_line: Lackadaisical Security (The Operator) - Spectre (GPT) - Aurora (Sonnet) - STONEDRIFT 3000
notes.resource_expansion_scope: metadata/resource-level enrichment plus per-entry source_refs; not a substitute for manual line-by-line philological collation.

morphology.notes: Attested in Codex Zographensis; from Proto-Slavic *bogъ

cultural_context.context_note: Glagolitic / Old Church Slavonic entries are intended for comparative script/lexicon work. Use source_refs and attested_resources for entry-level audit trails.
cultural_context.recommended_review: For publication-grade datasets, run a second pass that checks each lemma/sign against cited corpora and records exact inscription/manuscript identifiers.
